summary: The four classes of biomolecules are carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids.
The four classes of biomolecules are carbohydrates, lipids, vitamins, and minerals.
The four classes of biomolecules are proteins, nucleic acids, water, and electrolytes.
The fluid mosaic model describes the cell membrane as a phospholipid bilayer with embedded and peripheral proteins.
The fluid mosaic model describes the cell membrane as a rigid protein shell surrounding a lipid core.
The fluid mosaic model was proposed by Watson and Crick in 1953.
Lysosomes contain hydrolytic enzymes that function at acidic pH (~4.5) and their defects cause lysosomal storage diseases.
Lysosomes contain oxidative enzymes that function at alkaline pH (~8.5).
Lysosomes are responsible for ATP synthesis and energy production.
Mitochondria have their own DNA, show maternal inheritance, and are the site of oxidative phosphorylation.
Mitochondria show paternal inheritance and contain no DNA of their own.
Mitochondria are single-membrane organelles involved in protein folding.
The rough endoplasmic reticulum is studded with ribosomes and synthesises secretory and membrane proteins.
The rough endoplasmic reticulum is involved in lipid synthesis and detoxification.
The rough endoplasmic reticulum has no ribosomes and produces steroids.
The Golgi apparatus processes, packages, and sorts proteins received from the endoplasmic reticulum.
The Golgi apparatus is the site of DNA replication and transcription.
The Golgi apparatus directly synthesises all cellular proteins.
Peroxisomes contain oxidases that use molecular oxygen to detoxify harmful substances including hydrogen peroxide.
Peroxisomes are the main site of glycolysis in the cell.
Peroxisomes are identical to lysosomes and contain the same enzymes.
